name: Merrill Lynch Financial Advisor Brianna Roche
description_short: Merrill Lynch Private Wealth Management offers personalized financial advisory services through Managing Directors and Senior Vice Presidents at their New York office, serving high-net-worth clients with comprehensive wealth strategies.
description_long: Merrill Lynch's Private Executive Services division operates as part of the firm's Private Wealth Management practice, headquartered at 225 Liberty Street in New York, NY. The team includes multiple Managing Directors and Senior Vice Presidents with significant industry recognition and experience serving affluent clientele. The practice focuses on providing white-glove service to a limited number of clients, emphasizing personalized attention and customized strategies tailored to individual and family financial goals. This positioning reflects a deliberate focus on depth over breadth in client relationships. The advisory team leverages Merrill Lynch's institutional research capabilities combined with Bank of America's banking and lending solutions to create integrated wealth management plans. Services span investment management, financial planning, lending solutions, and banking conveniences through the parent company's ecosystem. The team members have been recognized by leading industry publications including Forbes, Barron's, and Financial Times, indicating strong professional standing within the wealth management community. Notable advisors include Andrew Kraus (Managing Director and Private Wealth Advisor), Robert McGinty (Managing Director and Private Wealth Advisor), Jeffrey M. Bryan (Resident Director and Managing Director), and Rachel Roberts (Senior Vice President and Private Wealth Advisor). The practice emphasizes understanding the client's full financial picture before developing strategies, positioning wealth management as an ongoing partnership rather than transactional advice. This is distinctly different from retail financial advisory; the minimum asset requirements and selective client acceptance indicate a focus on high-net-worth individuals and families. The website content reveals a practice oriented toward continuity planning and multi-generational wealth transfer, not immediate financial problem-solving or credit repair. While the firm operates under Merrill Lynch's regulatory framework and advisors are subject to FINRA oversight (BrokerCheck available), the specific focus is sophisticated wealth preservation and growth strategies for established affluent clients.
pros: Team members recognized by major industry publications (Forbes Best-in-State Wealth Advisors, Barron's Top 1200, Financial Times FT400)
Access to Merrill Lynch institutional research and investment insights combined with Bank of America banking and lending solutions
Limited client base approach enables personalized attention and customized strategies for each relationship
Multiple Managing Directors and Senior Vice Presidents provide continuity and depth of expertise
Comprehensive wealth management services covering investment, planning, lending, and banking in single relationship
Explicit focus on multi-generational wealth transfer and family financial planning
FINRA-regulated advisors with BrokerCheck transparency available to verify background
cons: No information on minimum asset requirements publicly disclosed, likely very high minimum that excludes most consumers
Website provides no pricing or fee structure transparency
Limited details on specific investment strategies, asset classes, or performance metrics
No information about advisory fees, whether fee-based, commission-based, or hybrid compensation
Website does not address conflict-of-interest policies or fiduciary obligations explicitly
best_for: High-net-worth individuals and families with $5M+ in investable assets seeking comprehensive wealth management
Executives and business owners requiring integrated financial planning including lending and succession planning
Multi-generational families focused on wealth preservation and transfer strategies
diagnosis: This company is a high-end private wealth advisory practice within Merrill Lynch, designed exclusively for affluent clients seeking sophisticated wealth management, not credit repair or financial distress solutions. The critical caveat is that this service is NOT appropriate for the credit-repair category and likely requires substantial minimum assets ($5M+) and selective engagement—prospective clients must contact directly to determine eligibility and discuss fees.
services: Family wealth transfer and succession planning
